rationale: The activity represents a clear certificate theft operation: A0 and EV-001 show certutil.exe exporting private keys to PFX files (Computer.pfx, test.pfx) using the -privatekey -exportpfx flags, EV-002 shows DC certificate store enumeration via -dcinfo, EV-003 enumerates the personal certificate store (-Store MY), and EV-004 exports a specific certificate by thumbprint to C:\cert.pfx (-exportPFX My 74ae941ce26523ae422d501e631decd0). This sequence — enumerate, identify, export — is consistent with credential/certificate harvesting (e.g., ADCS abuse or golden certificate attacks), all executed from a persistent cmd.exe session (PID 3532) under the Administrator account at high integrity. The staging of the PFX to C:\ root further suggests preparation for exfiltration.
